  source  = "app.terraform.io/meltwater/wrapidity-deployment/kubernetes"
  version = "1.0.24"

  service_name = var.atlas_backend_name
  service_version = "0.3.29"

  namespace = local.kubernetes_namespace

  internal_ingress_enabled = true
  additional_hostnames     = local.hostnames_to_enable
  whitelisted_cidrs        = join(", ", local.whitelisted_cidrs)

  env = var.k8s_env_name

  cpu          = "1000m"
  cpu_limit    = "2000m"
  memory       = "4096Mi"
  memory_limit = "4096Mi"

  min_replicas = 1 # TODO 1
  max_replicas = 1 # TODO 1

  max_surge       = "100%"
  max_unavailable = "50%"

  environment_variables = {
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID     = module.user.iam_key.id
    AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY = module.user.iam_key.secret

    AWS_SECRETSMANAGER_SECRET_NAME = data.terraform_remote_state.secrets.outputs.atlas_backend_properties.name
    SPRING_CLOUD_AWS_REGION_STATIC = var.aws_region

    # Import BOTH the properties secret (config: pulitzer URLs, news.app-id, ...)
    # and the api-keys secret. The properties secret was previously orphaned
    # (AWS_SECRETSMANAGER_SECRET_NAME was set but never consumed), so placeholders
    # like ${news.app-id} failed to resolve -> context startup failure. awspring
    # 3.x: one prefix, secrets separated by ';'.
    SPRING_CONFIG_IMPORT = "aws-secretsmanager:${data.terraform_remote_state.secrets.outputs.atlas_backend_properties.name};${data.terraform_remote_state.secrets.outputs.atlas_backend_api_keys.name}"

    SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL      = data.terraform_remote_state.postgres.outputs.atlas_backend_db.writer_jdbc_url
    SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME = trimspace(data.aws_kms_secrets.atlas_backend.plaintext["db_master_username"])
    SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD = trimspace(data.aws_kms_secrets.atlas_backend.plaintext["db_master_password"])

    CHATBOT_URL = "http://atlas-chatbot.${local.kubernetes_namespace}.svc.cluster.local:8000"

    # allowedApiKeys map is loaded via SPRING_CONFIG_IMPORT from the
    # api_keys SecretsManager secret (see api_keys.tf). Do NOT override
    # it here with SPRING_APPLICATION_JSON -- it has higher priority and
    # would wipe out all other API key entries.
  }
}

output "full_service_url" {
  value = module.deployment.service_url
}

  info_input_queue  = "${var.env_name}-info-${var.wraptor_name}-${var.default_input_queue}"
  info_output_queue = "${var.env_name}-info-${var.wraptor_name}-output${var.exchange-suffix}"
  version           = "2.7.1"
}

module "info_deployment" {
  source  = "app.terraform.io/meltwater/wrapidity-deployment/kubernetes"
  version = "1.0.22" #

  cooldown_period      = 900
  cpu                  = 1
  cpu_limit            = 2
  env                  = var.k8s_env_name
  internal_ingress_enabled    = true
  input_queue          = local.info_input_queue
  instance-class       = var.graviton_instance_type
  max_replicas         = 40
  memory               = "2G"
  memory_limit         = "3G"
  min_replicas         = 0
  namespace            = local.kubernetes_namespace
  keda_scaling_enabled = true
  queue_threshold      = 2
  service_name         = var.wraptor_name
  service_suffix       = "-info"
  service_version      = local.version

  environment_variables = {
    "AMQP_HOST"                   = "amqps://${local.service}-${var.env_name}:${local.rabbit_creds[local.service]}@${data.terraform_remote_state.rabbit.outputs.rabbit_host}:5671"
    "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"           = module.user.iam_key.id
    "AWS_REGION"                  = var.aws_region
    "AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"       = module.user.iam_key.secret
    "LOG4J_FORMAT_MSG_NO_LOOKUPS" = "true"
    "MESSAGE_STORAGE_BUCKET"      = "${var.env_name}-info-info"
    "MESSAGE_STORAGE_PREFIX"      = "components_output"
    "OPENAI_API_ENDPOINT"         = var.openai_hnts_url
    "OPENAI_API_KEY"              = data.aws_kms_secrets.openai_secrets.plaintext["password"]
    "OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME"      = var.openai_gpt_5_mini_wraptor
    "OPENAI_MAX_RETRIES"          = "1"
    "OPENAI_TEMPERATURE"          = "1.0"
    "OPENAI_TIMEOUT"              = "300"
    "POSTPROCESSING_HTTP_ENDPOINT"= "https://${data.terraform_remote_state.info_api.outputs.full_service_url}/v2/api/jobs/complete"
    "RABBIT_HOST"                 = data.terraform_remote_state.rabbit.outputs.rabbit_host
    "RABBIT_INPUT_QUEUE"          = local.info_input_queue
    "RABBIT_OUTPUT_QUEUE"         = local.info_output_queue
    "RABBIT_OUTPUT_QUEUE_ENABLED" = "true"
    "RABBIT_PASSWORD"             = local.rabbit_creds[local.service]
    "RABBIT_USERNAME"             = "${local.service}-${var.env_name}"
    "RABBIT_USE_SSL"              = "true"
    "SERVICE_NAME"                = "info-${var.wraptor_name}"
  }

  depends_on = []
}

output "full_service_url" {
  value = module.info_deployment.service_url
}

locals {
  service-suffix="${var.service_name}${var.service_suffix}"
}

resource "kubernetes_deployment" "deployment_with_scaling" {
  count = var.keda_scaling_enabled ? 1 : 0
  metadata {
    name      = local.service-suffix
    namespace = var.namespace
    labels    = {
      app                    = var.service_name
      MWBilling_CoE          = "FHAIPlatform"
      MWBilling_Environment  = "opex"
      MWBilling_Team         = "wrapidity"
      MWBilling_Department   = "410"
      FHAIPlatform_Component = "CrawlerInduction"
      MWBilling_Product      = "NewsCrawler"
      Contact                = "wrapidity-at-meltwater.com"
      Owner                  = "wrapidity"
      System                 = "pulitzer"
      Env                    = var.env
    }
  }
  spec {
    replicas = var.min_replicas
    strategy {
      #Bring up new pods _BEFORE_ taking down the old.
      rolling_update {
        max_surge       = var.max_surge
        max_unavailable = var.max_unavailable
      }
    }
    selector {
      match_labels = {
        app = local.service-suffix
      }
    }
    template {
      metadata {
        labels = {
          app                    = local.service-suffix
          MWBilling_CoE          = "FHAIPlatform"
          MWBilling_Environment  = "opex"
          MWBilling_Team         = "wrapidity"
          MWBilling_Department   = "410"
          FHAIPlatform_Component = "CrawlerInduction"
          MWBilling_Product      = "NewsCrawler"
          Contact                = "wrapidity-at-meltwater.com"
          Owner                  = "wrapidity"
          System                 = "pulitzer"
          Env                    = var.env
        }
        annotations = {
          "prometheus.io/scrape"         = var.prometheus_scraping_enabled
          "prometheus.io/path"           = var.prometheus_path
          "prometheus.io/port"           = var.service_port
          "logging.meltwater.io/system"  = "wrty-k8s"
          "logging.meltwater.io/service" = local.service-suffix
          "logging.meltwater.io/type"    = "nested_json"
          "harvest"                      = var.env
        }
      }
      spec {
        toleration {
          effect   = "NoSchedule"
          key      = "dedicated"
          operator = "Equal"
          value    = var.instance-class
        }
        node_selector = {
          "foundation.meltwater.io/instance-class" = var.instance-class
        }
        affinity {
          #Try to not put multiple pods on the same physical node
          dynamic pod_anti_affinity {
            for_each = var.anti_affinity == true ? [1] : []
            content {
            preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution {
              weight = 10
              pod_affinity_term {
                topology_key = "kubernetes.io/hostname"
                label_selector {
                  match_labels = {
                    app = local.service-suffix
                  }
                }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }

        dynamic "volume" {
          for_each = var.enable_rds_keystore_mount ? { vault_disabled = true } : {}
          content {
            name = "secrets"
            secret {
              secret_name = "rds-certificate-keystore"
              items {
                key = "keystore.jks"
                path = "keystore.jks"
              }
            }
          }
        }

        container {
          name  = local.service-suffix
          image = "${var.ecr_repo_base}/${var.service_name}:${var.service_version}"
          image_pull_policy = "Always"
          resources {

            limits = {
              cpu    = var.cpu_limit
              memory = var.memory_limit
            }

            requests = {
              cpu    = var.cpu
              memory = var.memory
            }
          }

          dynamic "volume_mount" {
            for_each = var.enable_rds_keystore_mount ? { vault_disabled = true } : {}
            content {
              mount_path = "/mnt/secrets"
              name       = "secrets"
              read_only  = true
            }
          }

          dynamic env {
            for_each = var.environment_variables
            content {
              name = env.key
              value = env.value
            }
          }
        }

        dynamic "init_container" {
          for_each = var.enable_dbinit ? { vault_disabled = true } : {}
          content {
            name  = "${local.service-suffix}-dbinit"
            image = "${var.ecr_repo_base}/${var.service_name}:${var.service_version}"
            command = var.dbinit_commands

            dynamic "volume_mount" {
              for_each = var.enable_rds_keystore_mount ? { vault_disabled = true } : {}
              content {
                mount_path = "/mnt/secrets"
                name       = "secrets"
                read_only  = true
              }
            }

            dynamic env {
              for_each = var.environment_variables
              content {
                name = env.key
                value = env.value
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }


  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes =   [
      spec.0.replicas, // ignored, because it is handled by auto-scaling
    ]
  }
}

resource "kubernetes_deployment" "deployment_without_scaling" {
  count = var.keda_scaling_enabled ? 0 : 1
  metadata {
    name      = local.service-suffix
    namespace = var.namespace
    labels    = {
      app                    = var.service_name
      MWBilling_CoE          = "FHAIPlatform"
      MWBilling_Environment  = "opex"
      MWBilling_Team         = "wrapidity"
      MWBilling_Department   = "410"
      FHAIPlatform_Component = "CrawlerInduction"
      MWBilling_Product      = "NewsCrawler"
      Contact                = "wrapidity-at-meltwater.com"
      Owner                  = "wrapidity"
      System                 = "pulitzer"
      Env                    = var.env
    }
  }
  spec {
    replicas = var.min_replicas
    strategy {
      #Bring up new pods _BEFORE_ taking down the old.
      rolling_update {
        max_surge       = var.max_surge
        max_unavailable = var.max_unavailable
      }
    }
    selector {
      match_labels = {
        app = local.service-suffix
      }
    }
    template {
      metadata {
        labels = {
          app                    = local.service-suffix
          MWBilling_CoE          = "FHAIPlatform"
          MWBilling_Environment  = "opex"
          MWBilling_Team         = "wrapidity"
          MWBilling_Department   = "410"
          FHAIPlatform_Component = "CrawlerInduction"
          MWBilling_Product      = "NewsCrawler"
          Contact                = "wrapidity-at-meltwater.com"
          Owner                  = "wrapidity"
          System                 = "pulitzer"
          Env                    = var.env
        }
        annotations = {
          "prometheus.io/scrape"         = var.prometheus_scraping_enabled
          "prometheus.io/path"           = var.prometheus_path
          "prometheus.io/port"           = var.service_port
          "logging.meltwater.io/system"  = "wrty-k8s"
          "logging.meltwater.io/service" = local.service-suffix
          "logging.meltwater.io/type"    = "nested_json"
          "harvest"                      = var.env
        }
      }
      spec {
        toleration {
          effect   = "NoSchedule"
          key      = "dedicated"
          operator = "Equal"
          value    = var.instance-class
        }
        node_selector = {
          "foundation.meltwater.io/instance-class" = var.instance-class
        }
        affinity {
          #Try to not put multiple pods on the same physical node
          dynamic pod_anti_affinity {
            for_each = var.anti_affinity == true ? [1] : []
            content {
            preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution {
              weight = 10
              pod_affinity_term {
                topology_key = "kubernetes.io/hostname"
                label_selector {
                  match_labels = {
                    app = local.service-suffix
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }

        dynamic "volume" {
          for_each = var.enable_rds_keystore_mount ? { vault_disabled = true } : {}
          content {
            name = "secrets"
            secret {
              secret_name = "rds-certificate-keystore"
              items {
                key = "keystore.jks"
                path = "keystore.jks"
              }
            }
          }
        }
        container {
          name  = local.service-suffix
          image = "${var.ecr_repo_base}/${var.service_name}:${var.service_version}"
          image_pull_policy = "Always"
          resources {
            requests = {
              cpu    = var.cpu
              memory = var.memory
            }

            limits = {
              cpu    = var.cpu_limit
              memory = var.memory_limit
            }
          }

          dynamic "volume_mount" {
            for_each = var.enable_rds_keystore_mount ? { vault_disabled = true } : {}
            content {
              mount_path = "/mnt/secrets"
              name       = "secrets"
              read_only  = true
            }
          }

          dynamic env {
            for_each = var.environment_variables
            content {
              name = env.key
              value = env.value
            }
          }
        }

        dynamic "init_container" {
          for_each = var.enable_dbinit ? { vault_disabled = true } : {}
          content {
            name  = "${local.service-suffix}-dbinit"
            image = "${var.ecr_repo_base}/${var.service_name}:${var.service_version}"
            command = [
              "java", "-jar", "/service/app.jar", "dbinit"
            ]

            dynamic "volume_mount" {
              for_each = var.enable_rds_keystore_mount ? { vault_disabled = true } : {}
              content {
                mount_path = "/mnt/secrets"
                name       = "secrets"
                read_only  = true
              }
            }

            dynamic env {
              for_each = var.environment_variables
              content {
                name = env.key
                value = env.value
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }

}

  version      = "13.12.0"
  service_name = var.ind_name
  input_queue  = "${var.env_name}-${var.ind_name}-${var.default_input_queue}"
  timeout      = 3000
}

module "deployment" {
  ecr_repo_base = "421213061221.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com"
  source  = "app.terraform.io/meltwater/wrapidity-deployment/kubernetes//modules/k8s-job"
  version = "1.0.16"


  cooldown_period             = local.timeout
  cpu                         = "1"
  cpu_limit                   = 4
  env                         = var.k8s_env_name
  input_queue                 = local.input_queue
  instance-class              = "memoryr54x"
  keda_scaling_enabled        = true
  # To scale up/down news-ind job check the instructions in the README.md file
  # https://github.com/meltwater/fhai-wrty-service-news-ind/blob/master/README.md
  max_replicas                = 30
  memory                      = "16G"
  memory_limit                = "19G"
  min_replicas                = 0
  namespace                   = local.kubernetes_namespace
  prometheus_scraping_enabled = true
  queue_threshold             = 1
  stabilizationWindowSeconds  = local.timeout
  service_name                = local.service_name
  service_version             = local.version


  environment_variables = {
    "AWS_REGION"                  = var.aws_region
    "DOMAIN"                      = var.induction_domain
    "DROP_REDELIVERIES"           = true
    "MAX_RAM"                     = "-XX:+UseG1GC -XX:InitialHeapSize=8g -XX:MaxRAMPercentage=85 -Dwrapidity.rabbit.outputExchange=${var.env_name}-induction-output.exchange -Dwrapidity.swallowErrors=false"
    "MESSAGE_STORAGE_BUCKET"      = "${var.env_name}-batch-frontier-news"
    "MESSAGE_STORAGE_PREFIX"      = "components_output"
    "POSTPROCESSING_LAMBDAS"      = "${var.env_name}-batch-pr-enrichment,${var.env_name}-batch-induction-finish"
    "TIMEOUT"                     = local.timeout
    "RABBIT_HOST"                 = data.terraform_remote_state.rabbit.outputs.rabbit_host
    "RABBIT_INPUT_QUEUE"          = local.input_queue
    "RABBIT_OUTPUT_QUEUE"         = ""
    "RABBIT_OUTPUT_QUEUE_ENABLED" = "false"
    "RABBIT_PREFETCH"             = 1
    "RABBIT_USE_SSL"              = "true"
    "S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET_NAME"       = "${var.env_name}-batch-frontier-news"
    "SERVICE_NAME"                = "batch-${var.ind_name}"

    "AMQP_HOST"             = "amqps://${local.service}-${var.env_name}:${local.rabbit_creds[local.service]}@${data.terraform_remote_state.rabbit.outputs.rabbit_host}:5671"
    "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"     = module.user.iam_key.id
    "AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY" = module.user.iam_key.secret
    "RABBIT_PASSWORD"       = local.rabbit_creds[local.service]
    "RABBIT_USERNAME"       = "${local.service}-${var.env_name}"
  }

  depends_on = []
}

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>I have a comprehensive picture. Here are my findings.

## 1. Manifest type: Terraform (not raw YAML / Helm / Kustomize)

Everything under `/Users/valeriocetorelli/IdeaProjects/wrapidity-terraform-prod/k8s/service` is **Terraform HCL** (`.tf` files). Kubernetes objects are created two ways:

- **Most services use a shared remote module** hosted on Terraform Cloud: `app.terraform.io/meltwater/wrapidity-deployment/kubernetes` (pinned versions like `1.0.24`). The per-service `deployment.tf` is just a thin `module` block passing CPU/memory/replicas/env vars. Examples: atlas_backend, wraptor, activator, delorean, highlighter, ipa, manutato, moonpie, nerd, pickle, sconfig_enhancer, source_config_service, urlbank, atlas_ui, info_api.
  - Some use submodule variants: `//modules/hnts-prod` (brioche, ext, page_segmenter), `//modules/hnts-sqs` (urlbank), `//modules/k8s-job` (induction — a KEDA ScaledJob).
- **A few services define raw `kubernetes_deployment` resources inline**: `atlas_chatbot` (`kubernetes_deployment_v1`), `mcp_postgres` (`kubernetes_deployment_v1` + a redis one), `browser-farm` (`kubernetes_deployment`).
- The only actual raw YAML is `nginx_ingress_controller/*.yaml` (ingress controller install), unrelated to the app workloads.
- The cached copy of the shared module is available locally at `brioche/.terraform/modules/deployment/deployment.tf` (and sibling copies), which reveals what the module actually renders.

The only non-Deployment workloads: `induction/job.tf` (KEDA ScaledJob via `k8s-job` module) and `source_config_pgcopydb/pod.tf` (a bare Pod — but **entirely commented out**).

## 2. Representative workload specs

### atlas_backend (shared module)
`atlas_backend/deployment.tf:1` — just a module call, no pod-template detail here:
- kind: Deployment (rendered by module), `service_version = "0.3.29"`, cpu 1000m/limit 2000m, memory 4096Mi, min/max replicas = 1, `max_surge = "100%"`, `max_unavailable = "50%"`.
- **No probes, no restart config** exposed here — the module doesn't render any.

### The shared module itself (`brioche/.terraform/modules/deployment/deployment.tf`)
This is what every module-based service actually gets:
- `kubernetes_deployment` (two variants: `deployment_with_scaling` when KEDA enabled, `deployment_without_scaling` otherwise), lines 6 and 180.
- `spec.strategy.rolling_update` with configurable `max_surge`/`max_unavailable` (lines 27-32), comment "Bring up new pods _BEFORE_ taking down the old."
- Pod template annotations (lines 54-62 / 228-236): only Prometheus scrape + Meltwater logging annotations + `harvest`. **No restart/redeploy annotation.**
- Container (line 108): image with `image_pull_policy = "Always"`, resources, env vars, optional keystore volume, optional dbinit init-container.
- **No `liveness_probe`, no `readiness_probe`, no `startup_probe`, no `restart_policy`, no `activeDeadlineSeconds`** in the module Deployment path.
- `lifecycle.ignore_changes = [spec.0.replicas]` (line 173-177) so autoscaling isn't fought by TF.

### mcp_postgres (raw, `mcp_postgres/deployment.tf`)
- `kubernetes_deployment_v1 "main"` (line 1). Full probe set:
  - `startup_probe` (line 149): `/actuator/health/liveness` on 8080, failure_threshold 30.
  - `liveness_probe` (line 159): same path, period 20, failure_threshold 3.
  - `readiness_probe` (line 168): `/actuator/health/readiness`, period 10.
  - `lifecycle.pre_stop` (line 141): `sleep 15` for connection draining.

### atlas_chatbot (raw, `atlas_chatbot/deployment.tf`)
- `kubernetes_deployment_v1 "main"`; `readiness_probe` (line 154) and `liveness_probe` (line 163) both hit `/health` on 8000.

### browser-farm (raw, `browser-farm/deployment.tf`)
- `kubernetes_deployment "main"`; `liveness_probe` (line 83) `/pressure?token=...` on 3000, `readiness_probe` (line 93) `/ready` on 3002. Uses KEDA (`browser-farm/keda.tf`) for scaling.

## 3. Scheduled pod restart pattern — DOES NOT EXIST

I found **no** mechanism in this repo to restart/recreate running pods on a schedule. Specifically searched for and did NOT find:
- No CronJob running `kubectl rollout restart` (no `kubernetes_cron_job` resource anywhere; no `null_resource`/`local-exec`/`kubectl` provisioners in any service `.tf` — the only `kubectl` hits are comments in `atlas_chatbot/data.tf:10` describing secret creation).
- No `kubectl.kubernetes.io/restartedAt` or any redeploy/restart annotation.
- No Reloader (`reloader.stakater.com`), no Descheduler, no Kured, no `checksum/config` rollout trigger.
- No `helm_release` / ArgoCD.
- No "max pod lifetime" annotation.

The only lifetime/deadline-adjacent things found are unrelated to scheduled restarts:
- `activeDeadlineSeconds = var.active_deadline` in the **k8s-job (KEDA ScaledJob)** module (`brioche/.terraform/modules/.../k8s-job/job.tf:79`) — a per-job execution timeout inside `jobTargetRef`, used by `induction`, not a Deployment restart.
- `failedJobsHistoryLimit`/`backoffLimit` in that same ScaledJob template.
- The many `*.cron` values found (e.g. `atlas_backend/properties.tf:115`, `ipa/deployment.tf:45` `UNSTUCK_CRON`, `pickle/deployment.tf:31-40`) are **application-level scheduling** passed as env/config to the apps (Spring `@Scheduled`-style), not Kubernetes pod restarts.
- `source_config_pgcopydb/pod.tf:31` `restart_policy = "Never"` is inside a fully commented-out Pod block.

## Summary
- Manifest type: **Terraform**, mostly via a shared `wrapidity-deployment/kubernetes` module; a handful of services (atlas_chatbot, mcp_postgres, browser-farm) declare raw `kubernetes_deployment` resources.
- Workloads are **Deployments** (plus one KEDA ScaledJob for `induction`); scaling via KEDA where enabled, rolling-update strategy standard.
- Probes exist only on the raw-resource services; the shared module renders **no probes and no restart controls**.
- **There is no existing scheduled/periodic pod-restart pattern** (no CronJob+rollout-restart, no Reloader/Descheduler, no restart annotations, no max-pod-lifetime). If you need to add memory-leak-mitigation restarts, you would be introducing a new pattern; the closest existing primitive is `activeDeadlineSeconds` on the KEDA job module, which only applies to the induction batch job.

Note: this is a read-only exploration, so I did not create a plan file or make any changes.</result>

## 1. Manifest type: Terraform (not raw YAML / Helm / Kustomize)

Everything under `/Users/valeriocetorelli/IdeaProjects/wrapidity-terraform-prod/k8s/service` is **Terraform HCL** (`.tf` files). Kubernetes objects are created two ways:

- **Most services use a shared remote module** hosted on Terraform Cloud: `app.terraform.io/meltwater/wrapidity-deployment/kubernetes` (pinned versions like `1.0.24`). The per-service `deployment.tf` is just a thin `module` block passing CPU/memory/replicas/env vars. Examples: atlas_backend, wraptor, activator, delorean, highlighter, ipa, manutato, moonpie, nerd, pickle, sconfig_enhancer, source_config_service, urlbank, atlas_ui, info_api.
  - Some use submodule variants: `//modules/hnts-prod` (brioche, ext, page_segmenter), `//modules/hnts-sqs` (urlbank), `//modules/k8s-job` (induction — a KEDA ScaledJob).
- **A few services define raw `kubernetes_deployment` resources inline**: `atlas_chatbot` (`kubernetes_deployment_v1`), `mcp_postgres` (`kubernetes_deployment_v1` + a redis one), `browser-farm` (`kubernetes_deployment`).
- The only actual raw YAML is `nginx_ingress_controller/*.yaml` (ingress controller install), unrelated to the app workloads.
- The cached copy of the shared module is available locally at `brioche/.terraform/modules/deployment/deployment.tf` (and sibling copies), which reveals what the module actually renders.

The only non-Deployment workloads: `induction/job.tf` (KEDA ScaledJob via `k8s-job` module) and `source_config_pgcopydb/pod.tf` (a bare Pod — but **entirely commented out**).

## 2. Representative workload specs

### atlas_backend (shared module)
`atlas_backend/deployment.tf:1` — just a module call, no pod-template detail here:
- kind: Deployment (rendered by module), `service_version = "0.3.29"`, cpu 1000m/limit 2000m, memory 4096Mi, min/max replicas = 1, `max_surge = "100%"`, `max_unavailable = "50%"`.
- **No probes, no restart config** exposed here — the module doesn't render any.

### The shared module itself (`brioche/.terraform/modules/deployment/deployment.tf`)
This is what every module-based service actually gets:
- `kubernetes_deployment` (two variants: `deployment_with_scaling` when KEDA enabled, `deployment_without_scaling` otherwise), lines 6 and 180.
- `spec.strategy.rolling_update` with configurable `max_surge`/`max_unavailable` (lines 27-32), comment "Bring up new pods _BEFORE_ taking down the old."
- Pod template annotations (lines 54-62 / 228-236): only Prometheus scrape + Meltwater logging annotations + `harvest`. **No restart/redeploy annotation.**
- Container (line 108): image with `image_pull_policy = "Always"`, resources, env vars, optional keystore volume, optional dbinit init-container.
- **No `liveness_probe`, no `readiness_probe`, no `startup_probe`, no `restart_policy`, no `activeDeadlineSeconds`** in the module Deployment path.
- `lifecycle.ignore_changes = [spec.0.replicas]` (line 173-177) so autoscaling isn't fought by TF.

- `kubernetes_deployment_v1 "main"`; `readiness_probe` (line 154) and `liveness_probe` (line 163) both hit `/health` on 8000.

- `kubernetes_deployment "main"`; `liveness_probe` (line 83) `/pressure?token=...` on 3000, `readiness_probe` (line 93) `/ready` on 3002. Uses KEDA (`browser-farm/keda.tf`) for scaling.

## 3. Scheduled pod restart pattern — DOES NOT EXIST

I found **no** mechanism in this repo to restart/recreate running pods on a schedule. Specifically searched for and did NOT find:

lastPrompt: look at my @../../k8s/service/ . Is there an option to tell k8s to delete and recreate a pod after x amount of time anyway?

content.text:
Got the full picture of your setup. Here's the direct answer.

## Short answer

**Kubernetes has no native "max pod lifetime / TTL" for pods managed by a Deployment.** A Deployment (which is what nearly everything in your `k8s/service` is) keeps its pods running indefinitely — there's no built-in field like "recreate every 6h." The `ttlSecondsAfterFinished` / `activeDeadlineSeconds` TTL features exist only for **Jobs**, not long-running Deployments.

That said, there are a few ways to get the effect. Ordered by how well they fit your repo:

### 1. CronJob that runs `kubectl rollout restart` (recommended)
A small `kubernetes_cron_job_v1` that periodically does `kubectl rollout restart deployment/<name>`. This does a **graceful rolling restart** — new pod up before old one drained, respects your `max_surge`/`max_unavailable`. Needs a ServiceAccount + Role/RoleBinding (patch on deployments). This is the standard pattern for "just recycle it every X hours" and works fine even for your single-replica services (like `atlas_backend`, min/max replicas = 1) because it brings up the replacement first.

### 2. `activeDeadlineSeconds` on the pod template (native, but blunt)
You *can* set `active_deadline_seconds` in the pod spec of a Deployment. When a pod exceeds it, the pod is killed (state `DeadlineExceeded`) and the ReplicaSet makes a new one. Downsides: it's **not graceful** — the pod is terminated hard, and for your **single-replica** services that means a brief outage each cycle. All replicas also tend to expire near-simultaneously. Generally discouraged for web/long-running workloads.

### 3. Descheduler `PodLifeTime` plugin (purpose-built, but new infra)
The [Descheduler](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/descheduler) has a `PodLifeTime` strategy that evicts pods older than `maxPodLifeTimeSeconds`. It's literally built for "recreate a pod after X time," but it means deploying and maintaining a new cluster component. Overkill unless you want it cluster-wide.

### What you have today
Your repo has **none** of these. There's no CronJob, no Reloader/Descheduler, no restart annotation anywhere. The only lifetime-adjacent thing is `activeDeadlineSeconds` on the KEDA **ScaledJob** used by `induction` — but that's a per-job execution timeout, not a Deployment recycler.

---

If this is about mitigating a **memory leak or slow degradation**, the CronJob rollout-restart (option 1) is what I'd add — it's graceful and safe for your 1-replica services. But the real fix is usually addressing the leak itself.

Do you want me to plan an implementation, and if so, which way?

questions.question: Do you want me to plan a periodic pod-restart implementation, and which approach?

options.label: CronJob rollout-restart
options.description: Add a kubernetes_cron_job_v1 + ServiceAccount/RBAC that runs `kubectl rollout restart` on a schedule. Graceful, safe for single-replica services. Recommended.

options.label: activeDeadlineSeconds
options.description: Add active_deadline_seconds to the pod template. Native, no extra infra, but ungraceful — causes brief downtime for single-replica services.

options.label: Descheduler PodLifeTime
options.description: Deploy the Descheduler component with a PodLifeTime policy to evict pods older than X. Purpose-built but adds a new cluster component.

options.label: Just the explanation
options.description: No implementation needed right now — the conceptual answer is enough.

questions.question: Which service(s) should the restart apply to?

options.label: One specific service
options.description: Apply it to a single Deployment (e.g. atlas_backend). I'll ask/confirm which one.

options.label: Several services
options.description: Apply the same pattern across multiple services.

options.label: Decide later
options.description: Figure out the target as part of planning — I'm still scoping it.
